content.subtitle: Form
content.description: Countable nouns have singular and plural forms and can take a singular or plural verb.
items: shop / shop**s** — There are over 100 **shops** in the new shopping centre.
baby / bab**ies** — They've got some great toys for **babies** in there.
dish / dish**es** — We need to get some new **dishes** for this evening.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We use these words with countable nouns: **a, an, many, a few, one, two, ...**

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: A few countable nouns have irregular plurals:
We cannot count some nouns (uncountable nouns). They do not have a plural form and take a singular verb, even if they end in **-s**.

rows: advice, bread, fruit, furniture, hair, homework, information, money, news, paper, rice, work

My **money** is in my wallet.
Your **hair** is really long!
The **news** was a complete shock.

content.description: We use these words with uncountable nouns:
We use these words with both countable and uncountable nouns:
We can use **any** in questions and negative statements with both uncountable nouns and plural countable nouns:

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: There are a few uncountable nouns that are plural and are followed by a plural verb. Be careful with the following words:

notes.correct: **clothes** — Your clean **clothes** are on the bed.

notes: Some nouns are uncountable with one meaning and countable with another meaning.

notes.correct: Get me some **paper** when you go to the shops. (= a packet of paper to write on)
Get me a **paper** when you go to the shops. (= a newspaper)

items.sentence: advice
apple
rice
child
information
dish
work
tooth

sections.title: B: Complete with much, many, a little or a few
sections.instructions: Choose the best quantifier for each sentence.

items.sentence: How ...... homework have you got tonight?
There are only ...... students in the classroom.
We have ...... time before the lesson starts.
How ...... books did you buy?
I need ...... help with this exercise.
There weren't ...... eggs left in the fridge.
She has ...... friends in this town.
There isn't ...... milk in the bottle.

sections.title: C: a/an, some or any
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with a, an, some or any.

items.sentence: Can I have ...... information about the museum, please?
There isn't ...... bread left.
I bought ...... apple and ...... orange.
Have you got ...... money with you?
We need ...... new chairs for the classroom.
She's ...... honest person.
There isn't **much / many** traffic today.
I've got **a few / a little** books about history.
How **much / many** people came to the party?
I need **a few / a little** water.
We didn't buy **much / many** souvenirs.
There are **a little / a few** mistakes in your essay.

sections.title: E: Correct the mistakes
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake with countable/uncountable nouns. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: I need an advice about my project.
items.answer: I need some advice about my project.

items.sentence: There are much people in the shop.
items.answer: There are many people in the shop.

items.sentence: She bought a bread and two milks.
items.answer: She bought some bread and two bottles of milk.

items.sentence: How many homework have you done?
items.answer: How much homework have you done?

items.sentence: The news are very good today.
items.answer: The news is very good today.

items.sentence: I have a few furniture in my room.
items.answer: I have a little furniture in my room.

items.sentence: Could I have ...... water, please?
How ...... chairs are there in the room?
There isn't ...... sugar in my coffee.
I've got ...... good friends at school.
My jeans ...... new.
Can you give me ...... piece of paper?

sections.title: G: Key word transformations
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given.

items.sentence:
I don't have many books. **FEW**
I only have ...... books.

There isn't much milk left. **LITTLE**
There is only ...... milk left.

Could you give me advice? **SOME**
Could you give me ...... ?

The trousers are expensive. **PAIR**
That ...... is expensive.

sections.title: H: Complete the text
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: We didn't have ...... food at home, so we went shopping.
We bought ...... apples and ...... bananas.
There wasn't ...... bread, so we got two loaves.
Mum gave me ...... money to buy some rice.
At the shop, there were ...... people waiting to pay.
I also bought ...... bottle of water.
Now we have ...... food for dinner.
